div1.entryFree: Manifest, vb. to make appear, to show plainly, to reveal: Wiv. IV, 6, 15. Meas. IV, 3, 94. V, 417. Ado III, 2, 100. Tw. II, 5, 181 (she --s herself to my love). H4B IV, 5, 105. Cor. II, 2, 14. Oth. I, 2, 32 (my parts, my title and my perfect soul shall m. me rightly). Partic. --ed adjectively: to make you understand this in a --ed effect, Meas. IV, 2, 169, i. e. so as to make the matter manifest, as to leave no doubt.
